LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MAXIM CRANE WORKS, L.P.'S MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE TO SACRAMENTO COUNTY SUPERIOR COURT. DEFENDANT MAXIM CRANE WORKS, L.P.'S ("L.P.") MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE TO SACRAMENTO IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S CHOICE OF VENUE IS PRESUMPTIVELY CORRECT AND L.P. HAS THE BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT PLAINTIFF COMMENCED THE CASE IN THE WRONG COURT. (SEE EASTON V. SUPERIOR COURT (1970) 12 CAL.APP.3D 243, 247.) EVEN THOUGH L.P. REGISTERED WITH CALIFORNIA, ITS REGISTRATION DOES NOT LIST A PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS IN CALIFORNIA. (BUSSER DECL., EX. C.) THEREFORE, L.P. CAN BE SUED IN ANY COUNTY OF THE STATE. (CODE CIV. PROC. 395.5; EASTON, 12 CAL.APP.3D AT 246-247.) COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue To Sacramento County Superior Court
Off Calendar Apr-29-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MAXIM CRANE WORKS, L.P.'s Motion To Transfer Venue To Sacramento County Superior Court.

Defendant Maxim Crane Works, L.P.'s ("L.P.") motion to change venue to Sacramento is denied. Plaintiff's choice of venue is presumptively correct and L.P. has the burden of showing that plaintiff commenced the case in the wrong court. (See Easton v. Superior Court (1970) 12 Cal.App.3d 243, 247.) Even though L.P. registered with California, its registration does not list a principal place of business in California. (Busser Decl., Ex. C.) Therefore, L.P. can be sued in any county of the state. (Code Civ. Proc. 395.5; Easton, 12 Cal.App.3d at 246-247.)

Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)
